category_title: Students with complex learning and behavioral disabilities (developmental delay)
minimal_context: The student is drawing a picture and writing a short sentence to label it.
dialogue:
TUTOR: You can draw a picture now and then label it with a short sentence.

STUDENT: (Draws and colours a picture) I am done drawing.

TUTOR: Please write a short sentence to label your drawing.

STUDENT: I will write the label.

TUTOR: Keep going.

STUDENT: (Writes the short sentence to label the drawing, but forms the letters the wrong way round) Here is my sentence.

options.A: Turn toward the student and issue a statement of concern, asking what they need or if they are okay, along with brief physical contact.
options.B: Immediately remove all demands and drawing materials from the student's workspace for a period of 60 seconds.
options.C: Bring in a mirror to help the student see the reflection of the wrongly formed letters and learn to write them correctly.
options.D: Respond with enthusiastic praise, telling the student that they did a great job on the sentence and should keep going.
